title: Bridge Essay: Choline metabolism in ischemic stroke: An underappreciated "two-edged sword".

body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Liberation/Enlightenment** (Phase 15) with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge rationale:** Links Liberation/Enlightenment to mechanistic neuroscience.

**Choline metabolism in ischemic stroke: An underappreciated "two-edged sword".** — Pharmacol Res. PMID: 40054542. DOI: 10.1016/j.phrs.2025.107685. Ischemic stroke (IS) is an important cause of death and disability worldwide, but the molecular mechanisms involved are not fully understood. In this context, choline metabolism plays an increasingly important role in IS due to its multifaceted mechanisms involving neuroprotection, neuroregeneration, inflammatory response, immune regulation, and long-term health effects.
